title: lac-lens — VS Code Extension
problem: Feature provenance context is most useful at the moment a developer is reading or editing code — not in a separate terminal or browser tab. Without editor integration, the feature.json is just a file the developer has to remember to open manually.
analysis: VS Code extension that surfaces feature provenance inline — CodeLens above files, hover tooltips, a sidebar tree view, a rich webview panel, and a status bar indicator. Works in two modes: standalone (filesystem walking) and enhanced (connected to lac-lsp over stdio). The extension auto-detects whether the LSP binary is available and switches modes silently. Commands cover the full feature lifecycle from the command palette.
implementation: Activated in extension.ts, which registers CodeLensProvider, HoverProvider, and the Explorer tree (FeatureTreeProvider). FeatureWalker.ts and FeatureCache.ts handle feature discovery and caching without the LSP. When lac-lsp is available, LacLspClient.ts takes over for richer diagnostics. FeaturePanel.ts renders a webview (HTML, not React) for viewing/editing feature details. All commands (createFeature, showFeaturePanel, changeStatus, addDecision, exportMarkdown) are in src/commands/. Built with esbuild to a single out/extension.js.

decisions.decision: Two-mode architecture: standalone filesystem + optional LSP
decisions.rationale: Most users won't run a persistent LSP server. Standalone mode works out of the box; LSP mode is an upgrade for power users who want diagnostics.
alternativesConsidered: LSP required — too much setup friction for casual users
Always filesystem — misses diagnostics and cross-file intelligence

decisions.decision: Webview panel as static HTML, not a React SPA
decisions.rationale: Simpler bundle, faster startup. The panel is read-heavy; full React reactivity isn't needed.
alternativesConsidered: React in webview — more interactive but heavier build pipeline inside the extension

knownLimitations: Standalone mode re-indexes features on every activation — can be slow in large repos
Webview panel is one-way HTML: rich interactions (inline editing) require page reload
If both standalone and LSP modes are active simultaneously, features can be double-indexed with stale data
Auto-detection of lac-lsp binary is silent — users don't know which mode they're in
successCriteria: Installing the extension from the Marketplace and opening a repo with feature.json files shows CodeLens annotations above relevant files, a populated sidebar tree, and working hover tooltips — with zero configuration.

lineage.spawnReason: Editor integration is a first-class surface of the LAC ecosystem alongside the CLI and web app.
